files.README.md:
# Greeter CLI

Greeter is a command-line application that prints a greeting for a supplied name.
The `--shout` option uppercases the greeting.
files.src/greeter.py:
import argparse

def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("name")
    parser.add_argument("--shout", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    greeting = f"Hello, {args.name}!"
    print(greeting.upper() if args.shout else greeting)

title: Greeter CLI

# Greeter CLI

Greeter is a command-line tool whose `--shout` flag uppercases the greeting. [Summary](repo:README.md#L3-L4)

The entry point uses `argparse` to collect arguments and print the result. [Implementation](repo:src/greeter.py#L1-L9)

files.README.md:
# Orders Service

The HTTP service exposes a health endpoint and order endpoints.
Requests flow through a router into handlers backed by an in-memory OrderStore.

pages.index.md:
---
title: Orders Service

# Orders Service

This repository is an HTTP service with a health endpoint and order operations. [Overview](repo:README.md#L3-L4)

Read the [architecture](architecture.md) and [request flow](request-flow.md).
pages.architecture.md:
---
title: Architecture
---
# Architecture

The server delegates every request to a router. [Server](repo:src/server.ts#L1-L5)

Order handlers share an in-memory `OrderStore`. [Store](repo:src/store.ts#L1-L5)

Return [home](index.md).
pages.request-flow.md:
---
title: Request Flow
---
# Request Flow

The router handles the health endpoint plus create and list order operations. [Branches](repo:src/router.ts#L3-L8)

The handlers delegate storage to `OrderStore`. [Handlers](repo:src/orders.ts#L1-L5)

Return to the [architecture](architecture.md).

structure: Python monorepo with applications, shared packages, features, and plugins

files.README.md:
# Event Platform

The platform has an API application, a worker application, shared packages, and plugins.
Commands become events on an event bus and are persisted through a repository protocol.
files.packages/storage/repository.py:
from typing import Protocol

class Repository(Protocol):
    def save(self, event: object) -> None: ...
    def load(self, event_id: str) -> object | None: ...
files.plugins/email/handler.py:
def send_email(payload: dict) -> None:
    assert payload["email"]
files.plugins/audit/handler.py:
def write_audit(payload: dict) -> None:
    assert "event_id" in payload

title: Event Platform

# Event Platform

The platform combines an API application, a worker application, plugins, an event bus, and a repository protocol. [Overview](repo:README.md#L3-L4)

Read the [architecture](architecture.md), [runtime](runtime.md), or [event flow](event-flow.md).
pages.architecture.md:
---
title: Architecture
---
# Architecture

The API application and worker application both receive the shared event bus. [API](repo:apps/api/main.py#L1-L4) [Worker](repo:apps/worker/main.py#L1-L4)

Storage is behind a repository protocol. [Repository](repo:packages/storage/repository.py#L1-L5)

Return [home](index.md).
pages.runtime.md:
---
title: Runtime
---
# Runtime

The event bus dispatches named payloads to registered handlers. [Bus](repo:packages/runtime/bus.py#L1-L8)

The plugin registry maps stable names to plugin objects. [Registry](repo:packages/runtime/plugins.py#L1-L5)

Continue to the [event flow](event-flow.md).
pages.event-flow.md:
---
title: Event Flow
---
# Event Flow

Applications publish to the event bus, which invokes each matching handler. [Dispatch](repo:packages/runtime/bus.py#L4-L8)

Email and audit plugins provide focused side effects. [Email](repo:plugins/email/handler.py#L1-L2)

Return to [runtime](runtime.md).
